name: Introduction to Rocket Propulsion
description: Basic principles of rocket propulsion and rocket engines.

lessons.name: History of Rocketry
lessons.description: A brief history of rocketry and space exploration.

lessons.name: Rocket Propulsion Fundamentals
lessons.description: Basic principles of rocket propulsion.

lessons.name: Rocket Engine Types
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket engines.

name: Fuel and Combustion
description: Understanding rocket fuel, combustion processes, and efficiency.

lessons.name: Rocket Fuel
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket fuels.

lessons.name: Combustion Processes
lessons.description: Understanding combustion processes and efficiency.

lessons.name: Combustion Instability
lessons.description: Understanding combustion instability and mitigation.

name: Nozzle Design
description: Principles of rocket nozzle design and performance optimization.

lessons.name: Nozzle Design
lessons.description: Overview of different types of rocket nozzles.

lessons.name: Nozzle Performance
lessons.description: Understanding nozzle performance and efficiency.

lessons.name: Nozzle Optimization
lessons.description: Optimizing nozzle design for specific applications.

name: Fundamentals of Aerodynamics
description: Basic aerodynamic concepts and fluid dynamics principles.

lessons.name: Fluid Dynamics Basics
lessons.description: Pressure, velocity fields, and the continuity equation.

lessons.name: Lift and Drag Forces
lessons.description: How airfoil shape generates lift and the sources of drag.

lessons.name: Airfoil Design
lessons.description: Principles of airfoil geometry and performance parameters.

name: Subsonic and Supersonic Flow
description: Understanding subsonic and supersonic aerodynamic behaviors.

lessons.name: Subsonic Flow Regime
lessons.description: Characteristics of airflow at speeds below the speed of sound.

lessons.name: Transonic and Supersonic Transitions
lessons.description: Flow behavior near and beyond Mach 1, including shock waves.

lessons.name: Shock Waves and Expansion Fans
lessons.description: Formation and effects of oblique shocks and Prandtl-Meyer expansions.

name: Aerodynamic Heating
description: Study of aerodynamic heating and thermal protection systems.

lessons.name: Sources of Aerodynamic Heating
lessons.description: How friction and compression generate heat on high-speed vehicles.

lessons.name: Thermal Protection Systems
lessons.description: Materials and design approaches for managing extreme heat loads.

lessons.name: Re-entry Vehicle Heating
lessons.description: Aerodynamic heating challenges specific to atmospheric re-entry.

name: Spacecraft Structural Design
description: Fundamentals of designing spacecraft structures and materials selection.

lessons.name: Structural Load Analysis
lessons.description: Types of loads spacecraft experience during launch and on-orbit.

lessons.name: Materials for Space Applications
lessons.description: Selection criteria and properties of materials used in spacecraft structures.

lessons.name: Structural Testing and Verification
lessons.description: Methods for verifying structural integrity before launch.

name: Orbital Mechanics
description: Understanding orbital dynamics and mission planning.

lessons.name: Kepler's Laws and Orbital Parameters
lessons.description: Fundamental laws governing orbital motion and how to characterize orbits.

lessons.name: Orbital Maneuvers and Delta-V
lessons.description: Hohmann transfers, plane changes, and fuel budgeting for missions.

lessons.name: Mission Planning and Launch Windows
lessons.description: Timing launch opportunities and designing mission trajectories.

name: Spacecraft Systems Engineering
description: Overview of spacecraft systems and subsystems engineering.

lessons.name: Power Systems
lessons.description: Solar arrays, batteries, and power distribution on spacecraft.

lessons.name: Thermal Control
lessons.description: Passive and active techniques for managing spacecraft temperature.

lessons.name: Payload Integration
lessons.description: Interfacing scientific and mission payloads with spacecraft bus systems.

name: Organic Compound Foundations
description: Structure, bonding, and naming of organic molecules.

lessons.name: Carbon Bonding and Hybridization
lessons.description: sp, sp2, and sp3 hybridization and their effect on molecular geometry.

lessons.name: Functional Groups
lessons.description: Identifying and classifying key functional groups in organic chemistry.

lessons.name: IUPAC Nomenclature
lessons.description: Systematic naming rules for organic compounds.

name: Reaction Mechanisms
description: Understanding how and why organic reactions proceed.

lessons.name: Substitution Reactions
lessons.description: SN1 and SN2 mechanisms, stereochemistry, and reaction conditions.

lessons.name: Elimination Reactions
lessons.description: E1 and E2 pathways and competition with substitution.

lessons.name: Addition Reactions
lessons.description: Electrophilic and nucleophilic addition to double bonds.

name: Organic Synthesis and Spectroscopy
description: Planning synthesis routes and characterizing organic compounds.

lessons.name: Retrosynthetic Analysis
lessons.description: Working backward from a target molecule to plan a synthesis.

lessons.name: Spectroscopic Methods
lessons.description: Using IR, NMR, and mass spectrometry to determine structure.

lessons.name: Stereochemistry
lessons.description: Chirality, enantiomers, and stereochemical outcomes of reactions.

name: Coordination Chemistry
description: Metal complexes, ligands, and coordination theory.

lessons.name: Coordination Compounds and Nomenclature
lessons.description: Structure and naming of metal-ligand complexes.

lessons.name: Crystal Field Theory
lessons.description: How ligand fields split d-orbitals and influence properties.

lessons.name: Chelation and Stability
lessons.description: Chelate effect, stability constants, and complex formation equilibria.

name: Bonding and Symmetry
description: Molecular orbital theory and symmetry in inorganic chemistry.

lessons.name: Group Theory Basics
lessons.description: Symmetry operations, point groups, and character tables.

lessons.name: Molecular Orbital Theory
lessons.description: Constructing MO diagrams for inorganic and organometallic compounds.

lessons.name: Vibrational Spectroscopy
lessons.description: Using IR and Raman spectroscopy to characterize inorganic compounds.

name: Inorganic Synthesis and Applications
description: Synthesis techniques and real-world applications of inorganic chemistry.

lessons.name: Synthesis Methods
lessons.description: Common laboratory techniques for synthesizing inorganic compounds.

lessons.name: Catalysis
lessons.description: Homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysis using metal complexes.

lessons.name: Bioinorganic Chemistry
lessons.description: The role of metal ions in biological systems and enzyme function.

name: Thermodynamics
description: Energy, entropy, and equilibrium in chemical systems.

lessons.name: First and Second Laws
lessons.description: Enthalpy, entropy, and the direction of spontaneous processes.

lessons.name: Gibbs Free Energy
lessons.description: Predicting reaction spontaneity and equilibrium using ΔG.

lessons.name: Chemical Equilibrium
lessons.description: Equilibrium constants, Le Chatelier's principle, and reaction quotients.

name: Quantum Mechanics and Spectroscopy
description: Quantum models of molecular structure and spectroscopic techniques.

lessons.name: Wave Functions and the Schrödinger Equation
lessons.description: Quantum mechanical models for particles and atoms.

lessons.name: Atomic Orbitals and Electron Configurations
lessons.description: Quantum numbers, orbital shapes, and electronic structure.

lessons.name: Spectroscopic Techniques
lessons.description: IR, UV-Vis, and NMR methods for probing molecular structure.

name: Kinetics
description: Rates of chemical reactions and the factors that influence them.

lessons.name: Rate Laws and Reaction Orders
lessons.description: Determining rate expressions and interpreting experimental data.

lessons.name: Activation Energy and the Arrhenius Equation
lessons.description: Temperature dependence of reaction rates and transition state theory.

lessons.name: Reaction Mechanisms and Catalysis
lessons.description: Elementary steps, rate-determining steps, and catalytic cycles.

name: Elvish Languages
description: Linguistic structure and historical context of Sindarin and Quenya.

lessons.name: Introduction to Quenya
lessons.description: History, grammar, and phonology of the High-Elven tongue.

lessons.name: Introduction to Sindarin
lessons.description: Origins, structure, and common usage of the Grey-Elven language.

lessons.name: Tengwar Script
lessons.description: Reading and writing the Elvish script used across Middle-earth.

name: Dwarvish and the Black Speech
description: Khuzdul and the Black Speech: structure, origins, and cultural context.

lessons.name: Khuzdul Grammar and Vocabulary
lessons.description: The secret language of the Dwarves: roots, morphology, and known inscriptions.

lessons.name: Cirth Runes
lessons.description: The runic script used primarily by Dwarves and its variants.

lessons.name: The Black Speech of Mordor
lessons.description: Sauron's constructed language: its purpose, structure, and use in inscriptions.

name: Philology and Linguistic History
description: The evolution and cultural significance of Middle-earth's languages.

lessons.name: Language Families of Middle-earth
lessons.description: How the languages of Elves, Men, and Dwarves relate and diverged over time.

lessons.name: Inscriptions and Texts
lessons.description: Analyzing key inscriptions and written works found throughout Middle-earth.

lessons.name: Tolkien as Philologist
lessons.description: How Tolkien's academic background shaped the languages he invented.

name: Foundations of Inter-species Diplomacy
description: Core concepts of diplomacy and political relationships in Middle-earth.

lessons.name: Political Structures of Middle-earth
lessons.description: Governance systems of the Elves, Men, Dwarves, and the role of the Wizards.

lessons.name: Principles of Diplomacy
lessons.description: Core frameworks for negotiation, alliance-building, and conflict resolution.

lessons.name: Historical Alliances and Conflicts
lessons.description: Survey of major inter-species agreements and wars across the Ages.

name: Case Studies in Middle-earth Diplomacy
description: Detailed analysis of pivotal diplomatic events across Middle-earth's history.

lessons.name: The Council of Elrond
lessons.description: A diplomatic analysis of the council's composition, debate, and decision-making.

lessons.name: The Last Alliance of Elves and Men
lessons.description: Formation and execution of the most significant alliance in the Second Age.

lessons.name: Dwarves and Elves: A Fractured Relationship
lessons.description: Historical roots of tension between Dwarves and Elves and attempts at reconciliation.

name: War, Peace, and Leadership
description: The role of leadership and wisdom in navigating conflict and cooperation.

lessons.name: The War of the Ring as a Diplomatic Crisis
lessons.description: How the free peoples coordinated (or failed to) in the face of existential threat.

lessons.name: Leadership Archetypes in Middle-earth
lessons.description: Comparing leadership styles of Gandalf, Aragorn, Théoden, and Denethor.

lessons.name: Post-War Reconstruction and Peace
lessons.description: Diplomacy after the War of the Ring and the challenge of rebuilding inter-species trust.
